summary: axios typosquat injecting unused suspicious transitive dependencies
details: Package name `axiosqqq` is a clear typosquat of the popular `axios` HTTP client and republishes the legitimate axios bundle as a façade. However, package.json declares two extra runtime dependencies that the real axios does not declare and that the bundled code never `require()`s: `@caspianph/storyteller` (^1.1.12) and `math` (^0.0.3). Because they are not referenced anywhere in the source, their only effect is to be resolved and installed by npm when a victim runs `npm install axiosqqq`, pulling attacker-chosen code into the installer's dependency tree where any lifecycle hook or import-time side effect of those packages will execute. This is the dependency-confusion / lure-package delivery shape: a clean-looking wrapper around a trusted library whose purpose is to silently drag unrelated packages into the victim's install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
